title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLXIII. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
There was an old woman of Leeds
        Who spent all her time in good deeds;
            She worked for the poor
            Till her fingers were sore,
        This pious old woman of Leeds!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. CCLXIII. Gaffers And Gammers."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
